content:
11 min read
Just now

--

“Performance problems rarely announce themselves with exceptions. They quietly steal milliseconds until your users start noticing.”
Introduction
It started like any other weekday.
Our production dashboards were green.
The application had been running smoothly for months, handling thousands of API requests every minute without breaking a sweat. Infrastructure looked healthy, monitoring alerts were quiet, and our on-call engineers were enjoying what seemed like a routine day.
Then, around 11:15 AM, everything changed.
Customer support started receiving complaints.
“The website feels slow.”
“Checkout is taking forever.”
“The mobile app keeps spinning.”
At first, we assumed it was a temporary network issue.
But within minutes, our monitoring dashboards told a different story.
Average API response time had jumped from 45 ms to nearly 700 ms.
At peak traffic, some requests were taking over 1.2 seconds.
For a customer-facing application, that was unacceptable.
The Production Environment
This wasn’t a small application.
